differential_diagnoses: Juvenile nasopharyngeal angiofibroma (unlikely given age/sex, but considered due to vascularity)
next_steps: Immediate airway assessment and anesthesia consultation due to high risk of airway compromise
Contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance angiography (MRA) or CT angiography (CTA) to characterize tumor vascularity
Full systemic staging with PET-CT to evaluate for metastatic disease and identify potential biopsy sites
Excisional biopsy under general anesthesia with readiness for intraoperative management of significant hemorrhage
Immunohistochemical staining including myogenin, MyoD1, desmin, pan-cytokeratin, and CD138
Comprehensive coagulation panel to rule out underlying bleeding diathesis
Multidisciplinary review including head and neck surgery, pediatric/adult oncology, and radiation oncology
